origin.session_summary: Planned and built the v1.1 website slice (cross-tab, cumulative chart via Chart.js, stats panel), introduced a website app version (1.2.0) distinct from the engine's schema_version, verified with a real headless-browser render (not just tsc/build), fixed a real bug found during that verification, then committed and pushed to main. User confirmed the live deploy looks correct.

do_not_redo.item: Chart library decision: Chart.js + vue-chartjs (official Vue 3 wrapper), chosen explicitly by the user over a hand-rolled SVG. Do not revisit unless the user raises it.

Test-claim gap (docs/TODO.md's "unit-tested 11 cases" claim for useHoursData's schema gate is NOT backed by any code - no test framework exists in website/): user explicitly decided to flag-only, fix later. Do NOT add vitest/tests and do NOT reword the original 2026-07-19 Done-log claim as part of routine work - a note was added to Later/parked instead. Only touch this if the user asks.
CrossTab has no totals row/column - deliberately minimal per WEBSITE_PLAN §4's "start minimal" instruction. Trivial to add later if the user wants it, but not a gap to "fix" unasked.
Website version (package.json / __APP_VERSION__) is a DIFFERENT number from the engine's schema_version (currently 1.1.0). Never conflate the two or bump one when asked to bump the other.

Playwright/Chromium is now available for real browser verification in this container (see domain_state) - required a one-time sudo pacman install of missing shared libs. Don't re-diagnose the libatk-1.0.so.0 error if it recurs; just re-run the same install.

decision.why: User's explicit choice over a hand-rolled SVG when asked; adds the project's first non-Vue/Tailwind/Vite dependency.

decision.what: Leave a "check later" note in docs/TODO.md Later/parked; do not fix the wording or add real tests now.
decision.why: User's explicit choice when asked; kept this slice's scope to exactly the three components plus the two follow-on requests.

decision.what: Introduce a website app version, independent of the engine's schema_version. Bumped to 1.2.0 (package.json), single source of truth read by vite.config.ts and injected as build-time constant __APP_VERSION__, shown in the page footer ("Website v1.2.0 · Schema 1.1.0 · ...").
decision.why: User request, made after approving the v1.1 plan: "bump the version to 1.2 ... a good idea to put a footnote in the website that this is vX.Y of the website ... easier to just glance and see what version is live."

decision.what: This repo has no staging/preview deploy - push to main IS the deploy trigger (.github/workflows/deploy.yml). Local check (npm run dev/preview) happens before commit+push, not after.
decision.why: Clarified for the user when they initially assumed a deploy-then-check-then-commit order that doesn't match this repo's actual CI wiring.

current_state.item: Branch main == origin/main at 6b98f8e (pushed and confirmed by user: build succeeded, footer shows v1.2, new sections render). One further tiny docs fix is UNCOMMITTED as of this snapshot - see warnings.
website/package.json version is 1.2.0. Engine's web_data.json schema_version is still 1.1.0, unrelated, untouched.
Three new components live: CrossTab.vue, CumulativeChart.vue, StatsPanel.vue, wired into App.vue after IntegrityPanel, in that order.
New dependencies in website/package.json: chart.js, vue-chartjs. No other new dependencies.

plan.md STATUS: EXECUTED 2026-07-21 (this slice). Old v1 plan archived at notes/plans/2026-07-19_website-v1-components.md; this session's plan.md itself still needs archiving at the next wrap-up (per convention, dated notes/plans/2026-07-21_website-v1.1-components.md or similar - not yet done).

docs/TODO.md: "Now" list re-numbered (v1.1 components item removed, the two remaining items - website improvements / monthly & unpaid-hours, and scripts/update.sh - shifted to 1 and 2). Done log has a new 2026-07-21 entry. Later/parked has the new test-claim-gap note. "Last updated" header line corrected to 2026-07-21 - this edit is the one UNCOMMITTED change, see warnings.

Playwright is installed only in a scratch npm project at $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/pw-scratch from the PRIOR session's job directory - this is job-scoped temp storage and will not persist/be available in a future session's job dir. The system libraries installed via pacman (at-spi2-core, gtk3, nss, alsa-lib, libxkbcommon-x11) DO persist (system level), but Chromium itself is cached at ~/.cache/ms-playwright/ and playwright itself is NOT a website/ or repo dependency - a future session wanting to browser-test again will need to either re-run `npx playwright install chromium` (libs are cached, so the download-only path should work) and set up its own scratch npm-install-playwright directory, or (better) ask the user if playwright should become a proper devDependency this time instead of a throwaway scratch install.

next_steps.step: Commit and push the one remaining uncommitted change (docs/TODO.md's "Last updated" header line, corrected to 2026-07-21) - trivial one-line docs fix, was pending explicit user confirmation before this session ended. Ask the user first (a fresh session should not just push without asking, per this project's/user's standing discipline).

See docs/TODO.md Now list for the next substantive work: item 1 is "website improvements" (per-month breakdown, unpaid-hours tracking) - both need a fresh plan-gate and both likely require engine schema 1.2.0 (a DELIBERATE lock-lift on engine_v2/, currently locked/ denied in .claude/settings.json - do not lift it casually). Item 2 is scripts/update.sh automation (BUILD_NOTES §5 caveats).
Archive this session's plan.md to notes/plans/ (dated) once the next slice's planning starts, per the project's own plan-archiving convention (see do_not_redo/current_state above).

files_touched.file: band × class matrix, minutesToHours on all cells
content.statistics, applies BUILD_NOTES §3 field→formatter map incl. both traps
minuteToClock now rounds first (bug fix - mean_start/end_minute are non-integer)
imports + wires the 3 new components after IntegrityPanel; footer now shows "Website v{{ appVersion }}"
reads package.json version, defines __APP_VERSION__ at build time
lockfile for the two new deps
rewritten for the v1.1 slice, then marked EXECUTED 2026-07-21
archived the prior (v1) EXECUTED plan before overwriting plan.md
v1.1 item moved to Done log with detail; test-claim-gap note added to Later/parked; Now list renumbered; header line corrected (THIS LAST EDIT IS UNCOMMITTED)

files_to_read.file: the ledger - start here; one uncommitted line pending (see warnings)
this session's EXECUTED plan, full success-criteria detail for what was built
§4 (v1.1 spec, now done) and whatever section covers the next Now item
§3 field→formatter map - load-bearing for any future stats/data-display work, has the trap categories (clocks vs durations vs counts vs percentages)
now includes the minuteToClock rounding fix; the only arithmetic surface

domain_state.website: App version 1.2.0 (website/package.json), independent of the engine's schema_version (1.1.0, in web_data.json). Nine components now: six v1 (SummaryHeader, TotalsPanel, WeeklyTable, DailyTable, MethodologyPanel, IntegrityPanel) + three v1.1 (CrossTab, CumulativeChart, StatsPanel). Dependencies: vue, tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/vite, chart.js, vue-chartjs. Deployed via .github/workflows/deploy.yml on push to main - NO separate staging; local npm run dev/preview is the only pre-deploy check available.
domain_state.verification-tooling: Playwright (chromium-headless-shell) is now launchable in this container after `sudo pacman -S at-spi2-core gtk3 nss alsa-lib libxkbcommon-x11` (was missing libatk-1.0.so.0 and friends). Browser binary cached at ~/.cache/ms-playwright/. Playwright itself was installed via a THROWAWAY scratch npm project outside the repo (this session's $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/pw-scratch), specifically to avoid adding a test-tooling dependency to website/package.json without being asked. A future session needing this again should either repeat the scratch-install pattern or ask the user whether to formalise it.
domain_state.engine: Unchanged this session. Schema 1.1.0, locked (deny rules active in .claude/settings.json). Next lift = 1.2.0 (see docs/TODO.md Later/parked) - still a future, deliberate, separately-planned change.

warnings.warning: ONE FILE IS UNCOMMITTED as of this snapshot: docs/TODO.md's "Last updated" header line was corrected from 2026-07-19 to 2026-07-21 AFTER the v1.1 commit (6b98f8e) had already been pushed. It was a one-line docs fix caught by the session-snapshot skill's own audit step, done too late to fold into that commit. Confirm with the user before committing/pushing it - do not push silently in a fresh session without asking, even though it's trivial.

The Bash tool's cwd does NOT reliably persist a `cd` across tool calls in the way you'd expect from a single shell - each Bash invocation in this environment can reset to the repo root ("Shell cwd was reset to ..." was observed mid-session). Always `cd` explicitly (or use an absolute path) at the start of every command that depends on being inside website/, rather than relying on a prior `cd` having stuck.

This project has NO ESLint/Prettier/PostToolUse hook for website/ TypeScript/Vue files (confirmed: no eslint/prettier in package.json, no hook in .claude/settings.json). Unlike engine_v2's ruff hook, multi-step edits to .vue/.ts files are safe to split across tool calls without the "add usage before import lands" hazard - but don't assume this generalises to other projects.

gh CLI is not installed in this container - cannot check GitHub Actions run status directly from the shell. Verification of the actual deploy currently depends on the user checking the live URL themselves (they did, this session, and confirmed it looks correct).
vue-tsc / npm build commands MUST run from website/, not repo root (repeated from the prior snapshot - still true, still easy to get wrong via the cwd-reset issue above).
